armies.name: Operation Imperator
armies.playstyle: Intercessors hold terrain footprints while Vanguard Veterans and the Land Speeder strike. Eradicators punish Ork hordes at range.

regimentAbilities.name: Take and Hold
regimentAbilities.summary: Primary missions reward holding terrain objectives — a natural fit for Intercessors and the Ancient.
regimentAbilities.newPlayerHint: Good default for your first Incursion game from the box.

regimentAbilities.name: Purge the Foe
regimentAbilities.summary: Primary missions reward destroying enemy units while still scoring for board presence.
regimentAbilities.newPlayerHint: Pick this if you plan to hunt the Warboss with Vanguard Veterans.

enhancements.name: Ancient's Campaign Banner
enhancements.summary: Lean on the Ancient to boost Objective Control around your Intercessors.
enhancements.newPlayerHint: Pairs with Take and Hold missions.
enhancements.timing: List building — 1 of 2 Enhancements at 1,000 pts

enhancements.name: Chaplain Leads the Assault
enhancements.summary: Attach the Jump Pack Chaplain to Vanguard Veterans for a decisive melee punch.
enhancements.newPlayerHint: Strong when you expect to charge early.
enhancements.timing: List building — 1 of 2 Enhancements at 1,000 pts

enhancements.name: Librarian's Psychic Support
enhancements.summary: Keep the Librarian with Intercessors for ranged control and utility.
enhancements.timing: List building — 1 of 2 Enhancements at 1,000 pts

enhancements.name: Captain at the Front
enhancements.summary: Your Relic Shield Captain anchors the line and protects key units.
enhancements.timing: List building — 1 of 2 Enhancements at 1,000 pts

armies.name: Waaagh! Armageddon
armies.playstyle: Boyz flood objectives while characters and the Dakkarig deliver the killing blow. Gretchin screen and the Wartrakk add ranged pressure.

regimentAbilities.name: Purge the Foe
regimentAbilities.summary: Primary missions reward krumping enemy units — matches an aggressive Ork play style.
regimentAbilities.newPlayerHint: Natural pick for a first game: chase the Space Marine characters.

regimentAbilities.name: Take and Hold
regimentAbilities.summary: Primary missions reward holding terrain objectives with your Boyz mobs.

enhancements.name: Warboss's Mighty Waaagh!
enhancements.summary: Keep the Warboss with Boyz to lead the central push.
enhancements.newPlayerHint: Safest first pick for melee pressure.
enhancements.timing: List building — 1 of 2 Enhancements at 1,000 pts

enhancements.name: Weirdboy with the Mob
enhancements.summary: Attach the Weirdboy to a Boyz unit for psychic support.
enhancements.timing: List building — 1 of 2 Enhancements at 1,000 pts

enhancements.name: Painboy's Patch-Up
enhancements.summary: Painboy keeps key units fighting after heavy damage.
enhancements.timing: List building — 1 of 2 Enhancements at 1,000 pts

enhancements.name: Dakkarig Spearhead
enhancements.summary: Build around the Big Mek Dakkarig as your firepower centerpiece.
enhancements.timing: List building — 1 of 2 Enhancements at 1,000 pts

matchSteps.title: Choose Armies
matchSteps.summary: Each player picks a faction and army list.
matchSteps.body: For the Armageddon box, use the fixed Operation Imperator and Waaagh! Armageddon rosters — about 1,000 points per side. Other factions can be added in future app updates with your own lists.
tips: Tap Use Starter Matchup for the Armageddon box armies.
Characters attach to units during list building in matched play.

matchSteps.title: Roll for First Turn
matchSteps.summary: Winner chooses who takes the first turn.
matchSteps.body: Roll off. The winner chooses which player takes the first turn. Unlike Spearhead, there is no separate attacker/defender for list building — but going first still matters for tempo.
tips: Going second can score primaries at the end of your turn on battle round 5 in many missions.

matchSteps.title: Pick Force Dispositions
matchSteps.summary: Each player chooses a mission posture for the game.
matchSteps.body: Your Force Disposition and your opponent's determine the Chapter Approved mission matchup. Pick one disposition each — Take and Hold, Purge the Foe, and others are tied to your detachments in full matched play.
tips: Armageddon players often pick Take and Hold for Marines and Purge the Foe for Orks — but either works.
Your primary objectives may differ from your opponent's.

matchSteps.title: Pick Enhancements
matchSteps.summary: Two Enhancements per player at 1,000 points.
matchSteps.body: At Incursion scale you typically take two Enhancements across your army. Choose options that match how you plan to use your characters and centerpiece units.
tips: Use your box datasheet cards for exact Enhancement rules.

matchSteps.title: Set Up the Battlefield
matchSteps.summary: Mission cards, terrain footprints, deployment, reserves.
matchSteps.body: Draw your Chapter Approved mission matchup, place terrain objectives from the map, deploy armies, then agree which units start off the board (Strategic Reserves or Deep Strike). Roll for first turn when deployment is complete.
tips: Objectives are terrain footprints in 11th Edition — not separate round markers.
Use the Chapter Approved deck from the Armageddon box.
Reserves cannot arrive in battle round 1. Units still off the board by end of round 3 are destroyed.

matchSteps.title: Fight the Battle
matchSteps.summary: Play five battle rounds — score victory points to win.
matchSteps.body: Play five battle rounds. Each turn: Command, Movement, Shooting, Charge, and Fight. Score primaries and secondaries from your mission cards, plus the Battle Ready bonus if your army is fully painted.
tips: Battle-shock tests happen in the Command phase.
Overwatch fires at the end of the enemy Movement phase.
